functions.name: refresh
functions.description:
Refresh the LCD screen

From 2.4.0 on color LCDs, this is done automatically when the screen
needs to be refreshed (on events and depending on refresh period).
functions.status: current Only used on non-color LCDs.

functions.name: clear
functions.description: Clear the LCD screen
notices: This function only works in stand-alone and telemetry scripts.
functions.status: current Introduced in 2.0.0, `color` parameter introduced in 2.2.0 RC12

functions.name: resetBacklightTimeout
functions.description: Reset the backlight timeout

functions.name: drawPoint
functions.description: Draw a single pixel at (x,y) position
notices:
Taranis has an LCD display width of 212 pixels and height of 64 pixels.
Position (0,0) is at top left. Y axis is negative, top line is 0,
bottom line is 63. Drawing on an existing black pixel produces white pixel (TODO check this!)

functions.name: drawLine
functions.signature: lcd.drawLine(x1, y1, x2, y2, pattern, flags)
functions.description: Draw a straight line on LCD
notices:
If the start or the end of the line is outside the LCD dimensions, then the
whole line will not be drawn (starting from OpenTX 2.1.5)
functions.status: current Introduced in 2.0.0, flags introduced in 2.3.6

functions.name: drawText
functions.description: Draw a text beginning at (x,y)

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description:
(unsigned number) drawing flags. All values can be
combined together using the + character. ie BLINK + DBLSIZE.
See the [Appendix](../appendix/fonts.md) for available characters in each font set.
`0 or not specified` normal font
`XXLSIZE` jumbo sized font
`DBLSIZE` double size font
`MIDSIZE` mid sized font
`SMLSIZE` small font
`INVERS` inverted display
`BLINK` blinking text
`SHADOWED` Horus only, apply a shadow effect

functions.name: drawTimer
functions.description: Display a value formatted as time at (x,y)

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description:
(unsigned number) drawing flags:
`0 or not specified` normal representation (minutes and seconds)
`TIMEHOUR` display hours
other general LCD flag also apply
`SHADOWED` Horus only, apply a shadow effect

functions.name: drawNumber
functions.description: Display a number at (x,y)

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description:
(unsigned number) drawing flags:
`0 or not specified` display with no decimal (like abs())
`PREC1` display with one decimal place (number 386 is displayed as 38.6)
`PREC2` display with tow decimal places (number 386 is displayed as 3.86)
other general LCD flag also apply
`SHADOWED` Horus only, apply a shadow effect

functions.name: drawChannel
functions.description: Display a telemetry value at (x,y)

parameters.name: source
parameters.description:
can be a source identifier (number) or a source name (string).
See getValue()

functions.status: current Introduced in 2.0.6, changed in 2.1.0 (only telemetry sources are valid)

functions.name: drawSwitch
functions.description: Draw a text representation of switch at (x,y)

parameters.name: switch
parameters.description:
number of switch to display, negative number
displays negated switch

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description: (unsigned number) drawing flags, only SMLSIZE, BLINK and INVERS.

functions.name: drawSource
functions.description: Displays the name of the corresponding input as defined by the source at (x,y)

functions.name: open
functions.description:
Loads a bitmap in memory, for later use with lcd.drawBitmap(). Bitmaps should be loaded only
once, returned object should be stored and used for drawing. If loading fails for whatever
reason the resulting bitmap object will have width and height set to zero.

Bitmap loading can fail if:
File is not found or contains invalid image
System is low on memory
Combined memory usage of all Lua script bitmaps exceeds certain value

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: (string) full path to the bitmap on SD card (i.e. “/IMAGES/test.bmp”)

returns.name: bitmap
returns.description: (object) a bitmap object that can be used with other bitmap functions

notices: Only available on Horus

functions.name: getSize
functions.description: Return width, height of a bitmap object

parameters.name: bitmap
parameters.description: (pointer) point to a bitmap previously opened with Bitmap.open()

returns.name: multiple
returns.description:
returns 2 values:
(number) width in pixels
(number) height in pixels

notices: Only available on Horus

functions.name: drawBitmap
functions.description: Displays a bitmap at (x,y)

parameters.name: bitmap
parameters.description: (pointer) point to a bitmap previously opened with Bitmap.open()

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description:
(positive numbers) scale in %, 50 divides size by two, 100 is unchanged, 200 doubles size.
Omitting scale draws image in 1:1 scale and is faster than specifying 100 for scale.

notices: Only available on Horus

functions.name: drawRectangle
functions.signature: lcd.drawRectangle(x, y, w, h [, flags [, t]])
functions.description: Draw a rectangle from top left corner (x,y) of specified width and height

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: thickness in pixels, defaults to 1 (only on Horus)

functions.name: drawFilledRectangle
functions.description: Draw a solid rectangle from top left corner (x,y) of specified width and height

functions.name: drawGauge
functions.signature: lcd.drawGauge(x, y, w, h, fill, maxfill [, flags])
functions.description: Draw a simple gauge that is filled based upon fill value

functions.name: setColor
functions.description: Set a color for specific area

parameters.name: area
parameters.description:
(unsigned number) specific screen area in the list bellow
`TEXT_COLOR`
`TEXT_BGCOLOR`
`TEXT_INVERTED_COLOR`
`TEXT_INVERTED_BGCOLOR`
`LINE_COLOR`
`SCROLLBOX_COLOR`
`MENU_TITLE_BGCOLOR`
`MENU_TITLE_COLOR`
`MENU_TITLE_DISABLE_COLOR`
`HEADER_COLOR`
`ALARM_COLOR`
`WARNING_COLOR`
`TEXT_DISABLE_COLOR`
`CURVE_AXIS_COLOR`
`CURVE_COLOR`
`CURVE_CURSOR_COLOR`
`TITLE_BGCOLOR`
`TRIM_BGCOLOR`
`TRIM_SHADOW_COLOR`
`HEADER_BGCOLOR`
`HEADER_ICON_BGCOLOR`
`HEADER_CURRENT_BGCOLOR`
`MAINVIEW_PANES_COLOR`
`MAINVIEW_GRAPHICS_COLOR`
`OVERLAY_COLOR`
`BARGRAPH1_COLOR`
`BARGRAPH2_COLOR`
`BARGRAPH_BGCOLOR`
`CUSTOM_COLOR`

parameters.name: color
parameters.description:
(number) color in 5/6/5 rgb format. The following prefined colors are available
`WHITE`
`GREY`
`LIGHTGREY`
`DARKGREY`
`BLACK`
`YELLOW`
`BLUE`
`RED`
`DARKRED`

notices: Only available on Colorlcd radios

functions.name: getColor
functions.description: Get the color for specific area : see lcd.setColor for area list
notices: Only available on Colorlcd radios

functions.name: RGB
functions.description: Returns a 5/6/5 rgb color code, that can be used with lcd.setColor

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: a number between 0x00 and 0xff that expresses te amount of red in the color

parameters.name: g
parameters.description: a number between 0x00 and 0xff that expresses te amount of green in the color

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: a number between 0x00 and 0xff that expresses te amount of blue in the color

returns.name: number
returns.description: (integer) rgb color expressed in 5/6/5 format

notices: Only available on Colorlcd radios

functions.name: getVersion
functions.description:
Return OpenTX version
### Example

This example also runs in OpenTX versions where the function returned only one value:

```lua
local function run(event)
  local ver, radio, maj, minor, rev, osname = getVersion()
  print("version: "..ver)
  if radio then print ("radio: "..radio) end
  if maj then print ("maj: "..maj) end
  if minor then print ("minor: "..minor) end
  if rev then print ("rev: "..rev) end
  if osname then print ("osname: "..osname) end
  return 1
end

return {  run=run }
```
Output of the above script in simulator:
```
version: 2.4.0
radio: tx16s-simu
maj: 2
minor: 4
rev: 0
osname: EdgeTX
```

returns.name: string
returns.description: OpenTX version (ie "2.1.5")

returns.name: multiple
returns.description:
values (available since 2.1.7):
(string) OpenTX version (ie "2.1.5")
(string) radio type: `x12s`, `x10`, `x9e`, `x9d+`, `x9d` or `x7`.
If running in simulator the "-simu" is added
(number) major version (ie 2 if version 2.1.5)
(number) minor version (ie 1 if version 2.1.5)
(number) revision number (ie 5 if version 2.1.5)
Since EdgeTX 2.4.0, sixth value added
(string) OS name (i.e. EdgeTX or nil if OpenTX)

functions.status: current Introduced in 2.0.0, expanded in 2.1.7, radio type strings changed in 2.2.0, os name added in EdgeTX 2.4.0

functions.name: getTime
functions.description: Return the time since the radio was started in multiple of 10ms

returns.name: number
returns.description:
Number of 10ms ticks since the radio was started Example:
run time: 12.54 seconds, return value: 1254

The timer internally uses a 32-bit counter which is enough for 497 days so
overflows will not happen.

functions.name: getDateTime
functions.description: Return current system date and time that is kept by the RTC unit

returns.name: table
returns.description:
current date and time, table elements:
`year` (number) year
`mon` (number) month
`day` (number) day of month
`hour` (number) hours
`hour12` (number) hours in US format
`min` (number) minutes
`sec` (number) seconds
`suffix` (text) am or pm

functions.name: getRtcTime
functions.description:
Return current RTC system date as unix timstamp (in seconds since 1. Jan 1970)

Please note the RTC timestamp is kept internally as a 32bit integer, which will overflow
in 2038.

returns.name: number
returns.description: Number of seconds elapsed since 1. Jan 1970

functions.name: getRotEncSpeed
functions.description: Return rotary encoder current speed

returns.name: number
returns.description:
in list: ROTENC_LOWSPEED, ROTENC_MIDSPEED, ROTENC_HIGHSPEED
        return 0 on radio without rotary encoder

functions.name: sportTelemetryPop
functions.description:
Pops a received SPORT packet from the queue. Please note that only packets using a data ID within 0x5000 to 0x50FF
(frame ID == 0x10), as well as packets with a frame ID equal 0x32 (regardless of the data ID) will be passed to
the LUA telemetry receive queue.

returns.name: nil
returns.description: queue does not contain any (or enough) bytes to form a whole packet

returns.name: multiple
returns.description:
returns 4 values:
sensor ID (number)
frame ID (number)
data ID (number)
value (number)

functions.name: sportTelemetryPush
functions.description:
This functions allows for sending SPORT telemetry data toward the receiver,
and more generally, to anything connected SPORT bus on the receiver or transmitter.

When called without parameters, it will only return the status of the output buffer without sending anything.

returns.name: boolean
returns.description: data queued in output buffer or not.

returns.name: nil
returns.description: incorrect telemetry protocol.

functions.status: current Introduced in 2.2.0, retval nil added in 2.3.4

functions.name: accessTelemetryPush
functions.description:
This functions allows for sending SPORT / ACCESS telemetry data toward the receiver,
and more generally, to anything connected SPORT bus on the receiver or transmitter.

When called without parameters, it will only return the status of the output buffer without sending anything.

returns.name: boolean
returns.description: data queued in output buffer or not.

functions.name: crossfireTelemetryPop
functions.description: Pops a received Crossfire Telemetry packet from the queue.

returns.name: nil
returns.description: queue does not contain any (or enough) bytes to form a whole packet

returns.name: multiple
returns.description:
returns 2 values:
command (number)
packet (table) data bytes

functions.name: crossfireTelemetryPush
functions.description:
This functions allows for sending telemetry data toward the TBS Crossfire link.

When called without parameters, it will only return the status of the output buffer without sending anything.

returns.name: boolean
returns.description: data queued in output buffer or not.

returns.name: nil
returns.description: incorrect telemetry protocol.

functions.status: current Introduced in 2.2.0, retval nil added in 2.3.4

functions.name: getFieldInfo
functions.description:
Return detailed information about field (source)

The list of valid sources is available:

| OpenTX Version | Radio |
|----------------|-------|
| 2.0 | [all](http://downloads-20.open-tx.org/firmware/lua_fields.txt) |
| 2.1 | [X9D and X9D+](http://downloads-21.open-tx.org/firmware/lua_fields_taranis.txt), [X9E](http://downloads-21.open-tx.org/firmware/lua_fields_taranis_x9e.txt) |
| 2.2 | [X9D and X9D+](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.2/release/firmware/lua_fields_x9d.txt), [X9E](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.2/release/firmware/lua_fields_x9e.txt), [Horus](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.2/release/firmware/lua_fields_x12s.txt) |
| 2.3 | [X9D and X9D+](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.3/release/firmware/lua_fields_x9d.txt), [X9E](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.3/release/firmware/lua_fields_x9e.txt), [X7](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.3/release/firmware/lua_fields_x7.txt), [Horus](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.3/release/firmware/lua_fields_x12s.txt) |

returns.name: table
returns.description:
information about requested field, table elements:
`id`   (number) field identifier
`name` (string) field name
`desc` (string) field description
'unit' (number) unit identifier [Full list](../appendix/units.html)

returns.name: nil
returns.description: the requested field was not found

functions.status: current Introduced in 2.0.8, 'unit' field added in 2.2.0

functions.name: getValue
functions.description:
Returns the value of a source.

The list of fixed sources:

| OpenTX Version | Radio |
|----------------|-------|
| 2.0 | [all](http://downloads-20.open-tx.org/firmware/lua_fields.txt) |
| 2.1 | [X9D and X9D+](http://downloads-21.open-tx.org/firmware/lua_fields_taranis.txt), [X9E](http://downloads-21.open-tx.org/firmware/lua_fields_taranis_x9e.txt) |
| 2.2 | [X9D and X9D+](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.2/release/firmware/lua_fields_x9d.txt), [X9E](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.2/release/firmware/lua_fields_x9e.txt), [Horus](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.2/release/firmware/lua_fields_x12s.txt) |
| 2.3 | [X9D and X9D+](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.3/release/firmware/lua_fields_x9d.txt), [X9E](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.3/release/firmware/lua_fields_x9e.txt), [X7](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.3/release/firmware/lua_fields_x7.txt), [Horus](http://downloads.open-tx.org/2.3/release/firmware/lua_fields_x12s.txt) |

In OpenTX 2.1.x the telemetry sources no longer have a predefined name.
To get a telemetry value simply use it's sensor name. For example:
Altitude sensor has a name "Alt"
to get the current altitude use the source "Alt"
to get the minimum altitude use the source "Alt-", to get the maximum use "Alt+"

parameters.name: source
parameters.description:
can be an identifier (number) (which was obtained by the getFieldInfo())
or a name (string) of the source.

returns.name: value
returns.description:
current source value (number). Zero is returned for:
non-existing sources
for all telemetry source when the telemetry stream is not received
far all non allowed sensors while FAI MODE is active

returns.name: table
returns.description:
GPS position is returned in a table:
`lat` (number) latitude, positive is North
`lon` (number) longitude, positive is East
`pilot-lat` (number) pilot latitude, positive is North
`pilot-lon` (number) pilot longitude, positive is East

returns.name: table
returns.description: GPS date/time, see getDateTime()

returns.name: table
returns.description:
Cells are returned in a table
(except where no cells were detected in which
case the returned value is 0):
table has one item for each detected cell:
key (number) cell number (1 to number of cells)
value (number) current cell voltage

notices:
Getting a value by its numerical identifier is faster then by its name.
While `Cels` sensor returns current values of all cells in a table, a `Cels+` or
`Cels-` will return a single value - the maximum or minimum Cels value.
functions.status:
current Introduced in 2.0.0, changed in 2.1.0, `Cels+` and
`Cels-` added in 2.1.9

functions.name: getRAS
functions.description: Return the RAS value or nil if no valid hardware found

returns.name: number
returns.description:
representing RAS value. Value bellow 0x33 (51 decimal) are all ok, value above 0x33 indicate a hardware antenna issue.
This is just a hardware pass/fail measure and does not represent the quality of the radio link

notices: RAS was called SWR in the past

functions.name: getTxGPS
functions.description: Return the internal GPS position or nil if no valid hardware found

returns.name: table
returns.description:
representing the current radio position
`lat` (number) internal GPS latitude, positive is North
`lon` (number) internal GPS longitude, positive is East
'numsat' (number) current number of sats locked in by the GPS sensor
'fix' (boolean) fix status
'alt' (number) internal GPS altitude in 0.1m
'speed' (number) internal GPSspeed in 0.1m/s
'heading'  (number) internal GPS ground course estimation in degrees * 10
'hdop' (number)  internal GPS horizontal dilution of precision

fields.name: lat
fields.description: internal GPS latitude, positive is North

fields.name: lon
fields.description: internal GPS longitude, positive is East

functions.name: getFlightMode

parameters.name: mode

flight mode number to return (0 - 8). If mode parameter
is not specified (or contains invalid value), then the current flight mode data is returned.

returns.name: multiple

returns 2 values:
(number) (current) flight mode number (0 - 8)
(string) (current) flight mode name

functions.name: playFile
functions.description: Play a file from the SD card

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
full path to wav file (i.e. “/SOUNDS/en/system/tada.wav”)
Introduced in 2.1.0: If you use a relative path, the current language is appended
to the path (example: for English language: `/SOUNDS/en` is appended)

functions.name: playNumber
functions.description: Play a numerical value (text to speech)

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: (number) number to play. Value is interpreted as integer.

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description:
(unsigned number) possible values:
`0 or not present` plays integral part of the number (for a number 123 it plays 123)
`PREC1` plays a number with one decimal place (for a number 123 it plays 12.3)
`PREC2` plays a number with two decimal places (for a number 123 it plays 1.23)

functions.name: playDuration
functions.description: Play a time value (text to speech)

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: number of seconds to play. Only integral part is used.

parameters.name: hourFormat
parameters.description:
:
`0 or not present` play format: minutes and seconds.
`!= 0` play format: hours, minutes and seconds.

functions.name: playTone
functions.description: Play a tone

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: length of the tone in milliseconds

parameters.name: pause
parameters.description: length of the silence after the tone in milliseconds

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description:
(number):
`0 or not present` play with normal priority.
`PLAY_BACKGROUND` play in background (built in vario function uses this context)
`PLAY_NOW` play immediately

parameters.name: freqIncr
parameters.description:
positive number increases the tone pitch (frequency with time),
negative number decreases it. The frequency changes every 10 milliseconds, the change is `freqIncr * 10Hz`.
The valid range is from -127 to 127.

functions.name: playHaptic
functions.description: Generate haptic feedback

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: length of the haptic feedback in milliseconds

parameters.name: pause
parameters.description: length of the silence after haptic feedback in milliseconds

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description:
(number):
`0 or not present` play with normal priority
`PLAY_NOW` play immediately

the event variable that is passed in from the
Run function (key pressed)

functions.name: popupConfirmation
functions.signature: popupConfirmation(title, event) deprecated, please replace by
functions.description: Raises a pop-up on screen that asks for confirmation

parameters.name: event
parameters.description:
the event variable that is passed in from the
Run function (key pressed)

returns.name: CANCEL
returns.description: user pushed EXIT key

notices: Use only from stand-alone and telemetry scripts.
functions.status: current Introduced in 2.2.0, changed to (title, message, event) in 2.3.8

functions.name: defaultStick
functions.description: Get stick that is assigned to a channel. See Default Channel Order in General Settings.

returns.name: number
returns.description: Stick assigned to this channel (from 0 to 3)

functions.name: setTelemetryValue
functions.signature: setTelemetryValue(id, subID, instance, value [, unit [, precision [, name]]])

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id of the sensor, valid range is from 0 to 0xFFFF

parameters.name: subID
parameters.description: subID of the sensor, usually 0, valid range is from 0 to 7

parameters.name: instance
parameters.description: instance of the sensor (SensorID), valid range is from 0 to 0xFF

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description:
the precision of the sensor
`0 or not present` no decimal precision.
`!= 0` value is divided by 10^precision, e.g. value=1000, prec=2 => 10.00.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
(string) Name of the sensor if it does not yet exist (4 chars).
`not present` Name defaults to the Id.
`present` Sensor takes name of the argument. Argument must have name surrounded by quotes: e.g., "Name"

returns.name: true,
returns.description: if the sensor was just added. In this case the value is ignored (subsequent call will set the value)

notices:
All three parameters `id`, `subID` and `instance` can't be zero at the same time. At least one of them
must be different from zero.

functions.name: defaultChannel
functions.description: Get channel assigned to stick. See Default Channel Order in General Settings

returns.name: number
returns.description: channel assigned to this stick (from 0 to 3)

returns.name: nil
returns.description: stick not found

functions.name: getRSSI
functions.description: Get RSSI value as well as low and critical RSSI alarm levels (in dB)

returns.name: rssi
returns.description: RSSI value (0 if no link)

returns.name: alarm_low
returns.description: Configured low RSSI alarm level

returns.name: alarm_crit
returns.description: Configured critical RSSI alarm level

functions.name: chdir
functions.description: Change the working directory

functions.name: loadScript
functions.description:
Load a Lua script file. This is similar to Lua's own [loadfile()](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#pdf-loadfile)
API method, but it uses OpenTx's optional pre-compilation feature to save memory and time during load.

Return values are same as from Lua API loadfile() method: If the script was loaded w/out errors
then the loaded script (or "chunk") is returned as a function. Otherwise, returns nil plus the error message.
### Example

```lua
  fun, err = loadScript("/SCRIPTS/FUNCTIONS/print.lua")
  if (fun ~= nil) then
     fun("Hello from loadScript()")
  else
     print(err)
  end
```

parameters.name: file
parameters.description:
(string) Full path and file name of script. The file extension is optional and ignored (see `mode` param to control
  which extension will be used). However, if an extension is specified, it should be ".lua" (or ".luac"), otherwise it is treated
  as part of the file name and the .lua/.luac will be appended to that.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description:
(optional) Controls whether to force loading the text (.lua) or pre-compiled binary (.luac)
  version of the script. By default OTx will load the newest version and compile a new binary if necessary (overwriting any
  existing .luac version of the same script, and stripping some debug info like line numbers).
  You can use `mode` to control the loading behavior more specifically. Possible values are:
`b` only binary.
`t` only text.
`T` (default on simulator) prefer text but load binary if that is the only version available.
`bt` (default on radio) either binary or text, whichever is newer (binary preferred when timestamps are equal).
Add `x` to avoid automatic compilation of source file to .luac version.
       Eg: "tx", "bx", or "btx".
Add `c` to force compilation of source file to .luac version (even if existing version is newer than source file).
       Eg: "tc" or "btc" (forces "t", overrides "x").
Add `d` to keep extra debug info in the compiled binary.
       Eg: "td", "btd", or "tcd" (no effect with just "b" or with "x").

parameters.name: env
parameters.description: See documentation for Lua function loadfile().

returns.name: function
returns.description: The loaded script, or `nil` if there was an error (e.g. file not found or syntax error).

returns.name: string
returns.description: Error message(s), if any. Blank if no error occurred.

notices:
Note that you will get an error if you specify `mode` as "b" or "t" and that specific version of the file does not exist (eg. no .luac file when "b" is used).
  Also note that `mode` is NOT passed on to Lua's loader function, so unlike with loadfile() the actual file content is not checked (as if no mode or "bt" were passed to loadfile()).

functions.name: resetGlobalTimer
functions.description: Resets the radio global timer to 0.

parameters.name: optional
parameters.description:
: if set to 'all', throttle ,throttle percent and session timers are reset too
                    if set to 'session', radio session timer is reset too
                    if set to 'ttimer', radio throttle timer is reset too
                    if set to  'tptimer', radio throttle percent timer is reset too

functions.status: current Introduced in 2.2.2, param added in 2.3

functions.name: multiBuffer
functions.description: This function reads/writes the Multi protocol buffer to interact with a protocol².

parameters.name: optional
parameters.description: value to write in the buffer

returns.name: buffer
returns.description: value (number)

parameters.name: baudrate
parameters.description:
Desired baurate

Set baudrate for serial port(s) affected to LUA

parameters.name: str
parameters.description:
String to be written to the serial port.

Writes a string to the serial port. The string is allowed to contain any character, including 0.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description:
(optional): maximum number of bytes to read.
                       If non-zero, serialRead will read up to num characters from the buffer.
                       If 0 or left out, serialRead will read up to and including the first newline character or the end of the buffer.
                       Note that the returned string may not end in a newline if this character is not present in the buffer.

returns.name: str
returns.description:
string. Empty if no new characters were available.

Reads characters from the serial port. The string is allowed to contain any character, including 0.

functions.name: getModule
functions.description:
Get RF module parameters

`Type` values:
0 NONE
1 PPM
2 XJT_PXX1
3 ISRM_PXX2
4 DSM2
5 CROSSFIRE
6 MULTIMODULE
7 R9M_PXX1
8 R9M_PXX2
9 R9M_LITE_PXX1
10 R9M_LITE_PXX2
11 R9M_LITE_PRO_PXX1
12 R9M_LITE_PRO_PXX2
13 SBUS
14 XJT_LITE_PXX2

`subType` values for XJT_PXX1:
-1 OFF
0 D16
1 D8
2 LR12

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: (number) module index (0 for internal, 1 for external)

returns.name: nil
returns.description: requested module does not exist

returns.name: table
returns.description:
module parameters:
`subType` (number) protocol index
`modelId` (number) receiver number
`firstChannel` (number) start channel (0 is CH1)
`channelsCount` (number) number of channels sent to module
`Type` (number) module type
if the module type is Multi additional information are available
`protocol` (number) protocol number (Multi only)
`subProtocol` (number) sub-protocol number (Multi only)
`channelsOrder` (number) first 4 channels expected order (Multi only)

fields.name: channelsCount
fields.description: number of channels sent to module

If a parameter is missing from the value, then

To set the `and` member (which is Lua keyword)

functions.name: getCurve
functions.description: Get Curve parameters

returns.name: nil
returns.description: requested curve does not exist

returns.name: table
returns.description:
curve data:
`name` (string) name
`type` (number) type
`smooth` (boolean) smooth
`points` (number) number of points
`y` (table) table of Y values:
`key` is point number (zero based)
`value` is y value
`x` (table) **only included for custom curve type**:
`key` is point number (zero based)
`value` is x value

 Note that functions returns the tables starting with index 0 contrary to LUA's
 usual index starting with 1

functions.name: setCurve
functions.description: Set Curve parameters

parameters.name: params
parameters.description:
see model.getCurve return format for table format. setCurve uses standard
 lua array indexing and arrays start at index 1

The first and last x value must -100 and 100 and x values must be monotonically increasing

returns.description:
- Everything okay
         1 - Wrong number of points
         2 - Invalid Curve number
         3 - Cuve does not fit anymore
         4 - point of out of index
         5 - x value not monotonically increasing
         6 - y value not in range [-100;100]
         7 - extra values for y are set
         8 - extra values for x are set

functions.status:
current Introduced in 2.2.0

Example setting a 4-point custom curve:
```lua
  params = {}
  params["x"] =  {-100, -34, 77, 100}
  params["y"] = {-70, 20, -89, -100}
  params["smooth"] = true
  params["type"] = 1
  val =  model.setCurve(2, params)
 ```
setting a 6-point standard smoothed curve
 ```lua
 val = model.setCurve(3, {smooth=true, y={-100, -50, 0, 50, 100, 80}})
 ```

functions.name: getGlobalVariable
functions.description: Return current global variable value

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: zero based global variable index, use 0 for GV1, 8 for GV9

returns.name: nil
returns.description: requested global variable does not exist

returns.name: number
returns.description:
current value of global variable

Example:

```lua
  -- get GV3 (index = 2) from Flight mode 0 (FM0)
  val = model.getGlobalVariable(2, 0)
```

notices: a simple warning or notice

functions.name: setGlobalVariable
functions.description: Sets current global variable value. See also model.getGlobalVariable()

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: zero based global variable index, use 0 for GV1, 8 for GV9

parameters.name: value
parameters.description:
new value for global variable. Permitted range is
from -1024 to 1024.

notices:
Global variable can only store integer values,
any floating point value is converted into integer value
by truncating everything behind a floating point.

constants.description: These are the theme colors plus , and they can be changed with the function lcd.setColor\(color_index, color\). Please note: if an indexed color is changed, then it changes everywhere that it is used. For the theme colors, this is not only in other widgets, but everywhere throughout the radio's user interface!
